embeds.description: No MO watchlist today, everything looks very very risky in my opinion. Will update you guys if I see anything. Best setup is PLRZ past 7.8 clean break but it would be very high risk because it has rejected lots. Be careful today.

You guys asked for the $QTTB play breakdown so here it is! The play is based on a strategy I learned called half dollar whole dollar breakouts! 

The idea is that you play the breakout based on psychological resistance breaking (using other people's mental to your advantage). 

You either want to play this a few cents above the break, or a bit before the break, I play before but I'm fairly good at reading the level 2 which makes this easier for me. I would not recommend doing this if you're copy trading or a beginner trader.

The trim levels were just 5%, then another half dollar psychological level which just also happened to be 10%, and then $6 which would be another psychological resistance.

Hope you guys enjoyed today's mini lesson! Keep watch for this strategy and you'll see it constantly play out.

Someone asked why I said clean break on $KTTA, so I shall deliver the explanation.

I said a clean break past $1.85 because $1.85 is clear resistance if you look at how many times price bounced off said level. If you look above the resistance you see a lot of wicking indicating strong seller presence. So by doing a **clean** break we minimize the chances of getting sold off on. The only risk you take is not being able to take the trade because it rips past said resistance. Hope this helps! React with a thumbs up if you enjoy these explanations!

embeds.title: рЯФЃ | TRADE UPDATE
embeds.description:
Today has been fairly bad, and it's unfortunate to say, but we are back in a cold market. So here's what you can do to keep your portfolio safe.

- Don't trade aggressively, what I mean by this is don't go for every single set up you see, trade carefully. Don't go for C or B setups, go for A+ setups only

- Trade with less shares or money, the market is a marathon, not a sprint

- Choose your stocks carefully, make sure they fit your criteria

- Shorten your trade sessions, get green and get out

- Prioritize risk management, calculate your RR

- Lastly, if you're a beginner and aren't profitable I would always recommend you to trade in a simulator, but this is especially true in a cold market.

If you guys wonder what my stock criteria is (what I look for daily) this is it below:

- Priced between $1-10 USD
- Preferably has news (sometimes stocks have momentum based off nothing but still move)
- Has a high relative volume
- Float under 10m (this can also be slightly extended to 20m float but I'll go smaller position)
- In the top 5 top gainers (Unless it's moving very quickly towards it with momentum)

All of this to say, please please please be safe in these conditions, I want to see every one of you here in the long run. Do not become part of the 95% statistic!
